channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok's parent firm could shun Nvidia, AMD as reports claim it will use 100,000 Huawei AI chips to train its next-gen LLM
item.description: Reports claim TikTok parent company ByteDance will use 100,000 Huawei AI chips to train its next-gen LLM.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: The secret to reading (yes, reading!) podcasts in your inbox
item.description: When we talk about email tools, we typically talk about apps or services that help you tame the wild jungle of your ever-overflowing inbox. Today’s tool is a twist. It’s about email, through and through—but it’s actually a smart new service that adds something to that environment.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Musk's Twitter Takeover: A Threat to Free Speech and Democracy?
item.description: Elon Musk's ownership of Twitter has raised concerns about the platform's impact on free speech, misinformation, and democracy.
item.dc:creator: Lorraine Ali, The Mercury News

item.title: Maintaining Security in the Digital Age: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday Initiative
item.description: Microsoft's monthly security update cycle, Patch Tuesday, provides crucial patches and fixes for various software products, ensuring users' safety in the digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump campaign, Musk coordinated to stop hacked material on X: Report
item.description: Former President Trump’s campaign worked with Elon Musk’s X to prevent links to hacked campaign materials from circulating on the social media platform last month, according to The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: MoneyGram hack exposes customers' sensitive data, cripples services
item.description: MoneyGram, an American payment and money transfer platform, confirmed a data breach that exposed sensitive customer data to hackers, forcing its services offline for five days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nube, ‘edge’ e IA: cómo sacar el máximo partido a este trinomio tecnológico
item.description: El mundo de TI está siendo testigo de una convergencia cada vez más cercana de la nube, el edge y la inteligencia artificial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why you should want face-recognition glasses
item.description: You start a conversation with someone, wondering if you’ve met them before. But then, your smart glasses tell you their name and where, when, and in what context you actually did meet them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ben Horowitz’s dual support for Trump and Harris says a lot about the Valley’s AI politics
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane’s impact on semiconductor industry remains a threat
item.description: One of two plants that mine high-purity quartz and silica sand needed for the production of semiconductors and other high-tech hardware has reopened operations after being shut down for more than a week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here's what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption beyond just the loss of a device.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton has caused thousands of flight cancellations, is yours one of them? What to know
item.description: Thousands of flights in and out of the U.S. have been canceled this week as Hurricane Milton barreled into the Gulf of Mexico and plowed across Florida.
item.dc:creator: Wyatte Grantham-Philips And David Koenig, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How a Cold War plan to stop nuclear proliferation could protect the world from an AI arms race
item.description: As Mark Twain said, “History doesn’t often repeat itself, but it does rhyme.” We are fortunate that it does, because it means we can use lessons from the past at least as a rough guide to the present.
item.dc:creator: Ash Jogalekar, Fast Company

item.title: If your agency is buying artificial intelligence, what are you exactly buying anyhow?
item.description: "General intellectual property law in the United States is still trying to figure out AI and a number of features of AI," procurement attorney Dan Ramish said. The post If your agency is buying artificial intelligence, what are you exactly buying anyhow? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: FinOps SaaS: The missing piece of the puzzle
item.description: With more and more businesses moving to the Cloud, FinOps is becoming a vital framework for efficiently controlling Cloud expenses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft’s Drasi: A lightweight approach to event-driven programming
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Incubations team is one of the more interesting components of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud. It is something between a traditional software development team and a research organization working to build solutions to the problems of distributed systems at massive scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI tracker charts growth in deepfakes ahead of election
item.description: The German Marshall Fund has launched a tool to track deepfakes created by artificial intelligence (AI) that are targeting elections and circulating in the U.S. and across the globe. The tool comes in a historic year for elections.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Twin boys born conjoined celebrate 1st birthday after separation surgery
item.description: Twin brothers who were born conjoined recently celebrated their first birthday after undergoing successful separation surgery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Planned Adobe app to put an end to AI training on images and clips
item.description: Adobe has unveiled plans for a new web app called Content Authenticity, which is part of the Content Credentials platform and will be used to ensure that AI models are not trained on content creators’ images, video, or audio clips without their permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk steps up Starlink access as Hurricane Milton approaches Florida
item.description: SpaceX and CEO Elon Musk ramped up the rollout of Starlink's direct-to-cell service for areas expected to be hit Wednesday by Hurricane Milton, marking the latest assistance efforts by the aerospace company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ransomware attack impacts 237,000 Comcast customers
item.description: Debt agency breach exposes 237,700 Comcast customers' personal data. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and provides six tips to help protect yourself from such a breach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Linus Torvalds annoyed by passive language in commit messages
item.description: The latest release candidate of Linux 6.12 was announced over the weekend, promoting some comments about grammar from Linux founder Linus Torvalds.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, The Hill

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: California’s vetoed AI bill: Bullet dodged, but not for long
item.description: Artificial intelligence has the power to revolutionize industries, drive economic growth, and improve our quality of life. But like any powerful, widely available technology, AI also poses significant risks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The best new features and fixes in Python 3.13
item.description: Python 3.13 has just been released. This article presents a rundown of the most significant new features in Python 3.13 and what they mean for Python developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up challenge to Jack Smith’s Trump Twitter data access
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a challenge by social platform X, formerly known as Twitter, to court rulings that forced the platform to turn over data on former President Trump’s account to special counsel Jack Smith.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: These are the 7 most devastating data breaches. Now what?
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson: Data breaches in 2024 exposed info of millions, stressing the need for better cybersecurity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
